channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: 100-Year Hurricane in NYC Could Cost Insurers More Than $100B: KCC
item.description: A 1-in-100-year hurricane event in New York City could cost insurers more than $100 billion, according to an analysis by Karen Clark & Co (KCC). And a 1-in-250-year hurricane could cost insurers twice as much, the catastrophe modeler says. The storm brought more than 15 feet of storm surge to Long Island and continued to track inland, causing damage well into Canada before transitioning to a post-tropical cyclone.

item.title: Chubb eyes Indian return as 100% FDI reshapes market access: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Agile promotes Mark Hunt to group CUO in underwriting leadership shake-up
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: XS Global, Sompo, Swiss Re, Marsh, Berkley Re: 14 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: HDH Investment Services Limited enters liquidation
item.description: On 16 April 2026, HDH Investment Services Limited (HDH), which advised on and arranged deals in investments, entered Creditors’ Voluntary Liquidation (CVL). If you do not hear from the joint liquidators or have more questions about your investment or pension, contact the Financial Services Compensation Scheme or the joint liquidators. Dina Devalia and Tom Parish of Quantuma Advisory Limited (Quantuma) have been appointed as joint liquidators.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Autonomous Vehicle Funding More Than Triples In 2026 To Hit Record Amount
item.description: Funding to autonomous vehicle startups has seen a massive resurgence in 2026, more than tripling so far this year compared to all of 2025 globally, Crunchbase data shows. So far in 2026, we’ve seen a shift to a small number of autonomous vehicle companies capturing a disproportionate share of global capital, with a handful of giants, including Waymo , Shield AI and Wayve , getting the lion’s share of funding. The numbers: Autonomous vehicle startups raised a record $21.4 billion across 34 deals through April 15, per Crunchbase data .

item.title: Investors show huge interest in data centre risk. Diversification story of ILS a consideration: SIFMA
item.description: Pawar stated, “It’s a complex subject matter, but I think, when we speak to our end-investor base, there’s a huge amount of interest in terms of this type of risk.” However, education is going to be critical as a new risk opportunity comes to the ILS market and Pawar highlighted that, “The kind of questions we’re being asked is, how are you looking at data centre risk, are you looking at cyber? During the SIFMA ILS conference in Miami yesterday a panel discussed the topic of data centre risk and some of the considerations that need to be kept in mind as the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market looks at these potentially meaningful opportunities. With the global data centre build-out continuing at pace and investments into their construction and financing growing, thoughts in the insurance and reinsurance market are on the enormous capacity opportunity to cover the significant risk limits being discussed, with ILS instruments seen likely to play a role, given the depths of capital market funds that could be deployed to support these facilities.

item.title: Turicum Re 2026-1 cat bond enables Zurich to re-establish its presence in growing ILS market: Mantero
item.description: Paolo Mantero, Head of Group Reinsurance for Zurich, commented: “Turicum Re enables Zurich to re-establish its presence and reputation in the growing and important ILS market. (Series 2026-1) issuance, which provides the firm with a multi-year and fully-collateralized source of US named storm and earthquake reinsurance protection, enables the company to re-establish its presence and reputation in the growing insurance-linked securities (ILS) market, Paolo Mantero, Head of Group Reinsurance has said. Initially, Zurich was looking to secure $125 million or more of multi-year and fully-collateralized US named storm and earthquake reinsurance protection from this Turicum Re 2026-1 cat bond deal.

item.title: Aon estimates Q1’26 global insured catastrophe losses of $20bn at least
item.description: Broking giant Aon has estimated that global insured catastrophe losses amounted to at least $20 billion in the first quarter of 2026, 6% above the 21st-century average, which came off the back of an economic toll of $37 billion, down sharply from $113 billion in the same period of 2025. Aon’s report also noted that natural catastrophes in the United States accounted for more than 75% of global insured losses in Q1 2026, reaching approximately $16 billion. According to the broker’s latest Global Catastrophe Recap, the largest contributors to the overall insurance toll were winter storms and severe convective storm (SCS) outbreaks across the United States.

item.title: Zurich closes $150m cat bond covering U.S. named storms and earthquakes
item.description: named storms and earthquakes, forming part of Zurich’s diversified natural catastrophe risk reinsurance strategy. Large insurer Zurich has returned to the catastrophe bond market, successfully closing a $150 million catastrophe bond covering U.S. named storms and earthquakes through the Turicum Re 2026-1 issuance .

item.title: Captive insurance identified as a financing mechanism supporting renewable energy growth: AXA XL
item.description: AXA XL, a global insurance and reinsurance company and part of the AXA Group, has published the report Future Energy Finance: How Captives are Powering Renewable Growth, which considers how the transition towards lower-carbon energy systems is increasing demand for adaptable risk financing approaches, with captive insurance arrangements highlighted as a key mechanism for supporting innovation and investment across the sector. The report also points to their role in enabling structured risk sharing across complex project arrangements, helping to align insurance and financial planning, as well as expanding available market capacity by supporting technologies as they progress from pilot schemes towards wider commercial deployment. Vicky Roberts-Mills, Global Head of Energy Transition at AXA XL, commented: “By 2030, global renewable capacity is expected to increase by 4,600 GW, with solar and wind technologies leading the charge, accounting for nearly 80% of new capacity.

item.title: Former Verisk CEO Stephenson joins ZestyAI’s Board of Directors
item.description: ZestyAI, a provider of AI-driven insurance risk analytics, has announced that Scott Stephenson, former Chairman, President, and CEO of Verisk Analytics, has joined its Board of Directors. Prior to Verisk, Stephenson was a Senior Partner at the Boston Consulting Group, where he advised Fortune 50 CEOs and founded the firm’s Southeastern U.S. Steph enson, who is considered one of the principal architects of the insurance industry’s data modernisation, is joining the company as the industry accelerates into the artificial intelligence era, ZestyAI highlights.

item.title: Europe Looks to Contain Trump’s Fury as It Hosts Hormuz Summit
item.description: The UK and France will host a summit to discuss setting up a naval force that would ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as Europe seeks to rebut US claims that it’s not doing enough to secure the waterway. Read More: US-Iran Deal Will Take Months, Gulf and Europe Officials Say The Paris meeting will be followed by a military planning summit in England next week and coupled with discussions with the insurance industry. The so-called Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative would be “strictly defensive in nature,” the UK said in a statement — underscoring how European leaders have no intention to help force a reopening of the Strait as President Donald Trump has demanded.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Will Take Months, Gulf and Europe Officials Say
item.description: Some Gulf Arab and European leaders believe that a US-Iran peace deal will take about six months to be agreed and that the warring sides should extend their ceasefire to cover that timeframe, according to officials from the regions familiar with the matter. Gulf states believe Iran is looking to build a nuclear weapon and that hasn’t changed in the wake of the US-Israeli bombardment of the country, said the officials. They thus think a peace deal should ban Iran from enriching uranium or having long-range ballistic missiles, the officials said.

item.title: Beazley Plans $1B Marine War Consortium for Transiting Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The consortium will be offered to vessels and their cargoes whilst transiting the Strait of Hormuz; coverage will be in line with Beazley’s risk appetite and in compliance with all global sanctions. Beazley, the specialty Lloyd’s insurer, has announced its intention to launch a marine war consortium, offering up to $1 billion of Lloyd’s capacity, which comprises $500 million for hull war and $500 million for cargo war. The consortium is designed to support the maritime sector with additional war insurance capacity as it deals with a complex and evolving situation in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gallagher expands UK footprint with Bridge Insurance Brokers acquisition
item.description: has expanded its UK presence with the acquisition of Bridge Insurance Brokers Limited, reinforcing its position in key specialty sectors including real estate and construction. By integrating Bridge into its UK & Ireland Retail division, the broker is enhancing its ability to serve mid-market organisations with tailored risk and insurance solutions. Bridge brings more than five decades of experience and a strong regional footprint, along with established expertise in complex commercial lines.

item.title: Inszone strengthens specialty capabilities with Randall Realty & Insurance acquisition
item.description: The post Inszone strengthens specialty capabilities with Randall Realty & Insurance acquisition appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Inszone Insurance Services has continued its expansion strategy with the acquisition of Randall Realty & Insurance, a move that enhances its presence in the specialised manufactured home insurance market. As demand for tailored insurance solutions continues to rise, the acquisition reinforces Inszone’s commitment to expanding its specialty capabilities while delivering enhanced value and resources to clients.

item.title: VisGen files trademark tied to insurance and denial recovery services
item.description: Visgen LLC has filed a trademark for “VISGEN,” signaling plans to expand into insurance and healthcare-focused services. Founded in 2025, VisGen is building an AI-driven denial recovery platform targeting healthcare providers, particularly federally qualified health centers. The application, filed April 14, 2026, covers insurance claims auditing, data processing, and consulting, including eligibility verification and health insurance advisory—pointing to a move beyond pure AI consulting into operational insurance workflows.

item.title: Inszone Insurance acquires Randall Realty & Insurance
item.description: Inszone Insurance has acquired Benchmark Insurance Group, which does business as Randall Realty & Insurance , adding a specialist in manufactured home insurance to its platform. We are excited to welcome the Randall Realty and Insurance team to Inszone and look forward to expanding the resources and support available to their clients.” – Chris Walters, CEO of Inszone Insurance Services. It really opens your eyes to see how smoothly a small business can integrate into a larger, well-structured business, and they made sure we had a clear understanding of what was happening every step of the way.” – Frank Fletcher, owner of Randall Realty and Insurance.

item.title: Luma launches life insurance platform
item.description: Luma Financial Technologies launched its life insurance platform, with Financial Independence Group serving as launch partner and first live client, as the company expands beyond annuities into live life insurance transactions. Now that platform is live and we’re bringing to market a modern life insurance workflow that helps firms operate more efficiently, support advisors more effectively, and manage business in a more consistent way.” – Tim Bonacci, CEO at Luma Financial Technologies. Luma stood out by delivering an intuitive, objective experience built around what our team needs most.” – David Henry, Executive Vice President, Life Insurance for Financial Independence Group.

item.title: Unespa participa en el Rendez-Vous de l’Assurance de Casablanca
item.description: La presidenta de Unespa, Mirenchu del Valle, ha participado e n la sesión inaugural del Rendez-Vous de l’Assurance de Casablanca , que concluyó ayer, y que organiza la Fédération Marocaine de l’Assurance, bajo la Presidencia de Mohamed Hassan Bensalah. Durante la sesión inaugural se firmó un acuerdo de cooperación entre la Fédération Marocaine de l’Assurance y la Asociación de Seguros de Omán (AOA), para potenciar el intercambio de experiencias y el fortalecimiento de la cooperación entre ambos mercados. Este destacó en su intervención de apertura las profundas transformaciones que se están produciendo en el sector, entre la aceleración tecnológica, la aparición de nuevos riesgos y la necesidad de preservar lo que es la base del seguro: la confianza.

item.title: Los gruistas piden a la DGSFP que intervenga para que las aseguradoras actualicen sus tarifas
item.description: Como adelantaron ayer nuestros colegas de Infotaller , la Alianza Nacional de Auxilio en Carretera ha elevado una consulta a la Dirección General de Seguros y Fondos de Pensiones (DGSFP) sobre el creciente riesgo en la viabilidad del servicio de grúas en España, ante el «incumplimiento sistemático» de las aseguradoras en actualizar las tarifas conforme al alza del precio del combustible , lo que podría afectar el servicio. Por ello, la Alianza insta a la DGSFP a auditar estas prácticas como parte de sus funciones de control y le pide que emita un informe o criterio interpretativo que clarifique el alcance de las prácticas de las aseguradoras desde el punto de vista del cumplimiento normativo. Recuerda al respecto que, según el artículo 38 de la Ley 15/2009 de contrato de transporte terrestre de mercancías, es imperativo que las tarifas se ajusten a la evolución del precio del combustible.

item.title: Eiopa propone reducir el contenido de 13 guías de Solvencia II
item.description: Eiopa ha lanzado una consulta pública para recabar opiniones sobre su propuesta de simplificar el marco de Solvencia II mediante la reducción del contenido de 13 guías supervisoras , con el objetivo de disminuir la carga administrativa del sector. En total, las guías objeto de revisión suman 294 directrices que Eiopa plantea acortar de forma significativa, en línea con su objetivo de reducir su extensión al menos un 25% cuando sea posible. El supervisor europeo subraya que la consulta se centra exclusivamente en la simplificación de las guías existentes y no contempla, por ahora, la introducción de nuevos requisitos.

item.title: Gallagher Bassett Names Memisevic Senior Vice President and Chief Architect
item.description: Darmin Memisevic has been promoted to senior vice president – chief architect of Gallagher Bassett. Memisevic will shape and build Gallagher Bassett’s infrastructure and solutions working to provide better client and claimant experiences. Memisevic joined Gallagher Bassett in 2015 as a consultant.

item.title: Three Sentenced in Videoed Bear-Suit Attacks Insurance Fraud Case
item.description: An insurance fraud scheme involving a bear costume, luxury vehicles and falsified damage claims has ended in convictions for three Los Angeles area residents. The CDI investigation discovered the defendants used a person in a bear suit to stage fake attacks on high-end vehicles, then submitted fraudulent claims seeking payouts from insurance companies. The CDI had a biologist from the California Department of Fish and Wildlife review the three alleged bear videos and the expert verified it was a human in a bear suit.

item.title: Camp Mystic Official Testifies Deaths Still Haven’t Officially Been Reported to State
item.description: The medical officer for the Texas summer camp where 27 girls were killed in a flood last year testified Tuesday she still has not officially reported the deaths to the state health agency that regulates camps and is reviewing its application to reopen this summer. While the deaths of 25 campers and two teenage counselors at the all-girls Christian camp have been widely reported and are not in question, the Texas administrative code requires camps to report deaths to state health regulators within 24 hours. Mary Liz Eastland, a member of the family that owns and operates Camp Mystic, was questioned in a legal fight between the camp operators and families of victims who have filed lawsuits and want the camp to preserve damaged areas as evidence.

item.title: IAG’s RACI acquisition moves to phase two ACCC investigation
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AI12 appoints Joseph Barness as Head of Marine Lines
item.description: Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) headquartered specialist insurance and reinsurance broker, AI12 Limited, has named Joseph Barness as Head of Marine, expanding its global specialty practice. “I’m joining AI12 to build a Marine Lines operation with conviction across Hull & Machinery, Marine Cargo, Marine & Cargo War Risks and P&I, supported by disciplined risk understanding, strong market engagement, and a service model that is responsive, technical and market-leading.” Anvar Mullabekov, Founder and Chief Executive Officer, AI12 Limited, commented, “Joseph’s appointment is another strategic step in AI12’s long-term growth plan and follows our recent DFSA authorisation in the DIFC. “Marine Lines spanning Hull & Machinery, Cargo, War Risks, P&I and related liabilities, is a core part of that strategy, and Joseph will play a key role in building a differentiated proposition, strengthening our practice with worldwide markets, and reinforcing AI12’s position as a leading specialist broker.”

item.title: AI adoption & governance accelerate across Lloyd’s market: LMA
item.description: The Lloyd’s Market Association (LMA), in collaboration with Barnett Waddingham and the LMA Risk Next Generation Committee, has published a survey finding that artificial intelligence (AI) is now used across much of the Lloyd’s market, with 93% of firms having or developing formal AI frameworks to support adoption. The survey is based on 39 responses from firms representing over 60% of Lloyd’s market stamp capacity. Twelve months on, AI is now widely used across the market, with 93% of respondents reporting that they have or are developing an AI framework—72% already in place and 21% in development.

item.title: Allstate estimates $1.24bn Q1’26 cat losses with March contributing $925m
item.description: For March 2026 alone, Allstate estimated catastrophe losses of $925 million, or $731 million after tax. The Allstate Corporation, a provider of personal property and casualty insurance in the US, has announced its estimated catastrophe losses for the first quarter of 2026, totalling $1.24 billion, or $980 million, after-tax. March 2026 losses include 15 wind and hail events; just three of these events were responsible for approximately 80% of the month’s total losses.

item.title: Nationwide: Consumers Say Insurance Should Evolve for Micromobility Vehicles
item.description: Almost all consumers agree that insurance policies should evolve to provide clearer coverage for micromobility vehicles on public roads, Nationwide shared in a new report. A majority of respondents to Nationwide’s survey believe that motorized scooters (81%) and off-road vehicles (80%) should require insurance coverage to operate on public roads. Among commercial drivers, 85% said policies should evolve to provide clearer coverage for the increasingly popular lightweight vehicles.

item.title: Reinsurers Least Successful Acquirers in Industry M&A: Analysis
item.description: A recently released analysis of insurance carrier and reinsurer mergers and acquisitions finds that 75% of recent deals featuring reinsurers and multiline insurers as buyers destroyed value for shareholders. The report, “Carrier Mergers & Acquisitions: Drivers, Implications & Outcomes,” published by the insurance industry standards-setting body ACORD , notes that publicly traded reinsurer and multiline (writing both property/casualty and life/health insurance) buyers in M&A deals stand apart from P/C and L/H carriers, who are largely value creators. 31, 2025, and indexed against the MSCI World Index to isolate deal-specific outcomes from broader equity market movements.) Related article : Nearly Half of 100 Largest P/C Insurers Destroy Value: ACORD As a group, reinsurers and multiline carriers produced 13.3% lower returns than the market between close date and year-end 2025, while post-deal returns for P/C insurers were 3.1% above the overall equity market.

item.title: Spain’s Blackout Probe Blames Grid Operator, Government, Watchdog
item.description: An inquiry by the Spanish Senate blames the government, as well the country’s grid operator and energy watchdog, for last year’s unprecedented blackout, according to preliminary conclusions made public on Wednesday. The probe by the upper house – which is controlled by the opposition People’s Party (PP) – is the first to assign blame for the outage that plunged large parts of Spain and Portugal into darkness for up to 16 hours on April 28, 2025. The Senate commission held grid operator Red Electrica, a unit of Redeia, and the Energy Ministry primarily responsible for the outage, while also criticizing energy and antitrust regulator CNMC for what it described as regulatory and supervisory inaction.

item.title: Cajas negras en el onboarding: la vulnerabilidad que crece en banca digital
item.description: “Si dependes de terceros… tienes cajas negras, y una caja negra es un punto débil”, explica. Además, como prueba de esta capacidad en entornos de máxima exigencia, Veridas es el primer motor biométrico facial instalado físicamente en los servidores del Instituto Nacional Electoral (INE) , garantizando la verificación directa y segura sin intermediarios ni cajas negras», añade Torres. En un entorno donde el fraude escala industrialmente, cualquier punto ciego en el proceso de verificación de identidad de bancos y fintechs puede convertirse en su principal vulnerabilidad operativa.

item.title: Applied Systems Names Graham Blackwell CEO
item.description: Applied Systems (Chicago) has named Graham Blackwell as Chief Executive Officer, with a leadership transition planned over the next two quarters. Graham Blackwell, President and CEO-designate, Applied Systems. “Insurance is at an AI-powered inflection point, and Applied stands in a unique position to drive the industry forward, to be the fabric that connects the business of insurance, and to use the power of AI to make the entire system work better,” says Graham Blackwell, incoming CEO, Applied Systems.

item.title: AI for the Defense: Should Insurers or Law Firms Pay?
item.description: Nor is the suggestion that defense firms need to try to catch up with similar ammo. The idea that AI tools can help plaintiffs’ lawyers to improve finely tuned litigation strategies that ultimately drive insurance company claims payouts is not new to Carrier Management readers.

item.title: Systemic risk is making an unwelcome return on the APAC radar
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Swiss Re appoints Devpriya Misra as APAC head of credit and surety reinsurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Applied Systems names Graham Blackwell as CEO, Rhodes to transition out
item.description: Applied Systems has named Graham Blackwell as its next chief executive officer, with current CEO Taylor Rhodes set to transition out over the next two quarters. Blackwell, who currently serves as president, will officially assume the role ahead of the company’s Applied Net 2026 conference in September. Prior to Applied, Blackwell held roles at SMS Assist and spent nearly eight years at Bain Capital’s private equity operating team.

item.title: Travelers reports record FQ1 2026 earnings
item.description: Travelers kicked off 2026 with record-breaking first-quarter results, driven by disciplined underwriting across all segments and a significant jump in investment income. Financial Highlights: FQ1 2026 Travelers demonstrated high margin retention despite a complex macroeconomic and geopolitical landscape. Following the strong performance, Travelers’ Board of Directors authorized a 14% increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $1.25 per share.

item.title: BP Faces Lawsuit Over 500 Deaths in Kenyan Exploration Area
item.description: A group of almost 300 petitioners filed a class-action suit against BP Plc and Kenyan authorities seeking compensation for environmental damage caused by disposal of toxic waste at oil-exploration sites in the northern fields of the East Africa nation. Nema, the Kenyan regulator, said the government environment policy was enacted years after the exploration activity. They allege the oil major disposed hazardous materials at drilling wells in northern Kenya, leading to the deaths of about 500 people and a spike in cancer cases, according to the petition filed at a court in Kenya.

item.title: Nord Stream Blasts Due to War, Say Insurers Seeking to Avoid Pay Out
item.description: The Nord Stream gas pipelines were attacked in 2022 as a direct result of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, insurers told a London court on Thursday as they seek to avoid paying out nearly 580 million euros ($684 million) over blasts that hit the pipelines. Related: Nord Stream Insurers Deny Policies Covered War Risks In UK Lawsuit Two Ukrainians have been arrested in Italy and Poland respectively at the request of German prosecutors after traces of explosives were found on a boat linked to the blasts. Insurers Fight Lawsuit Over 2022 Explosions Lawyers representing Lloyd’s and Arch say Nord Stream’s insurance policy excludes damage caused by war or under the order of any government.

item.title: House Approves Aviation Safety Bill Based on Deadly Midair Collision
item.description: An aviation safety bill seeking to address lessons learned from last year’s midair collision of a jet with an Army helicopter near the nation’s capital was approved by the House on Tuesday, but key senators and the families of the 67 victims think the bill still needs to be strengthened. “A warning to my colleagues in the House: the Alert Act would not deliver the safety measures necessary to prevent another midair collision, as it lacks the critical improvements our aviation system needs,” Cruz, the Texas Republican, posted on X ahead of the vote on Tuesday. The House bill, called the Alert Act, has the backing of key industry groups, but lawmakers will now have to try to find a compromise that will satisfy the Senate.

item.title: Britain Considers Expanding Ban on NDAs That Silence Workplace Abuse Complaints
item.description: Britain may widen a planned ban on non-disclosure agreements that silence victims of workplace harassment, discrimination, and abuse to include agency workers and the self-employed, as well as permanent staff, the government said on Wednesday. “We are committed to ending a culture of silence and impunity and stand with all survivors of harassment and abuse in the workplace,” Employment Rights Minister Kate Dearden said. “These changes will ensure no one has to suffer in silence and give workers confidence that inappropriate behavior will be dealt with.” Campaigners such as Zelda Perkins, founder of not-for-profit campaign group Can’t Buy My Silence, have for years campaigned against the misuse of NDAs, which they say perpetuate sexism and discrimination and prevent bosses from ending poor culture.

item.title: U.S. Sustained Storm-Related Insured Losses Over $50B Three Years in a Row: Triple-I
item.description: insured losses in 2025, marking the third straight year losses have exceeded $50 billion, according to a new members-only Issues Brief published by the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I). “Severe convective storms are no longer a ‘secondary’ regional or seasonal concern as recent years have proved they are a year-round, record-setting insured loss challenge,” said Sean Kevelighan, CEO, Triple-I. Notable weather events in 2025 included more than 300 tornadoes in March alone, which generated $8.4 billion in insured losses, and a rare EF5 tornado struck North Dakota in June, ending a 12-year absence of the most violent tornado classification in the United States.

item.title: Exclusive: GetWhys Raises $5.2M To Help Companies Like Intel And Verizon Better Understand Their Customers
item.description: Impressively, the startup’s customers include the likes of Intel , Verizon , Docusign , Mission Cloud (CDW) and Commvault . Companies can also connect their own internal intelligence — such as sales call transcripts — into its offering. When Boutros teamed up with college friends and former Intel software engineers Viet Phan and Tyler Honsinger to found GetWhys three years ago, he admits that it was “a rather different company.” “We thought we were building a knowledge base for B2B buyers,” Boutros recalls.

item.title: Nationwide Mutual returns with $200m target for Aquila Re I 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Nationwide Mutual Insurance Company is back in the catastrophe bond market with a target to secure $200 million or more in multi-peril reinsurance protection from the capital markets with a new Aquila Re I Ltd. The $200 million or more in reinsurance from this new Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond will protect Nationwide Mutual against losses from the perils of US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm, wildfire, meteorite impact, volcanic eruption, the same range of perils as its previous two cat bonds under the structure. In fact, this new Aquila Re I deal will be the eleventh Nationwide sponsored catastrophe bond issuance that we have listed in our extensive Deal Directory .

item.title: Insurers mull leveraging third-party capital to write more data centre business: GC’s Klisura
item.description: “I think the newest element of it… is clients now are talking about issuing cat bonds and leveraging third-party capital to write more data center business. And all of Guy Carpenter’s clients want to write more data centers, but they all need additional reinsurance protections.” As we’ve highlighted before, underwriting the data centre build-out, where major technology companies, hyperscalers, and artificial intelligence startups are investing hundreds of billions of dollars to create new capacity to support and deliver their computationally demanding software and services, is regarded as being one of the most significant new opportunities for re/insurers in recent decades. However, as Klisura observes, insurers are reportedly considering to leverage cat bonds and third-party capital to support this expansion.

item.title: Casualty sidecar / ILS market to expand. Alignment, maturity driving investor interest: SIFMA
item.description: In essence, the increasing maturity of this segment of the market is driving greater confidence, while the alignment inherent in the structures is helping fuel investor interest as they seek access to insurance-linked returns. The casualty insurance-linked securities and casualty reinsurance sidecar market is seen as primed for expansion, with speakers at today’s SIFMA ILS conference in Miami saying the aligned nature of the structures and the motivation of cedents to participate all suggest traction will persist. “What underwriters and insurers see here is an interesting opportunity in casualty to explicitly benefit from investment returns and yield, that are harder to achieve in the traditional reinsurance market.

item.title: Marsh CEO Doyle ‘pleased’ with Guy Carpenter’s execution in Q1’26 in spite of headwinds
item.description: John Doyle, President and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of global insurance and reinsurance broking group Marsh, said today that while 2026 is unlikely to be Guy Carpenter’s best growth year, he is “pleased” with the reinsurance broker’s execution in the first quarter in spite of current headwinds. “What I would say to you is that I’m quite pleased with our execution, in spite of the current good headwinds. Guy Carpenter recorded revenue of $1.2 billion in the first quarter of 2026 , a year-on-year increase of 3%, or 2% on an underlying basis, amid what Marsh CEO Doyle, during the firm’s recent earnings call, described as a very soft property catastrophe reinsurance market.
